parsed_review.summary: CP00-576 is a clean descriptor-only pack covering RP18.P07.M05.S01-S10 (Permission And Audit Binding; 9 failure_recovery + 1 security_audit at S08). Scope, deliverable distribution, row titles, and authority boundary are consistent across the closeout plan, review baseline, manifest, contract projection (cp576_requirements + p07_permission_slice_descriptor), registry pack binding, service/validator implementation, and tests. The pack-level production_ready claim is honest and supported: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor — no runtime is opened. All 10 rows are descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only=true/raw_payload_included=false; no fs/child_process/net/http imports exist in packages/ai-legal-workflows/src and builders are pure frozen-object factories, so no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold. The single security_audit row was inspected via full Read/Grep source review (summary-only correctly prohibited) and shows no permission-evaluation runtime, no raw payload, and no leak. Claude authority boundary is intact (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false), and Hermes appears only as a deterministic evidence-packet descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The validator deep-equals the regenerated descriptor against the committed contract, giving strong drift protection. Review evidence passes CP178+ hardening: auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1445-byte stdout; not a 0-byte/auth-failure/malformed case) and command-evidence records npm test, npm run validate, git diff --check, and all rp18/plan/weighted/spec/fullplan/goal/control-plane validations passing. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two P3 informational notes are non-blocking and expected by design. This read-only review is independent input and not final approval — human approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: Post-review closeout artifacts not yet present (expected by design)
findings.details: manifest.evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-576/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which do not yet exist in the worktree. This is expected: manifest.status is 'implemented_pending_review' and pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending'; these artifacts are produced by this review run plus the downstream hardening sequence (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation). It is not a defect in the descriptor implementation, but the pack is not closeout-complete until they exist and validate.
findings.recommendation: Ensure the post-review hardening steps generate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and that 'npm run closeout-pack:validate' confirms their presence before commit.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected/by-design; gated by closeout-pack:validate prior to commit. Non-blocking for the descriptor-implementation review.

findings.title: Worktree carries unrelated dirty/untracked files outside pack scope
findings.details: changed-file-scope.json records unrelated dirty/untracked files preserved outside the pack (e.g., scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html, and several workbook/* documents). These are not in active_pack_implementation_refs and are correctly excluded from CP00-576. git diff --check passed, but the worktree is broadly dirty, so an over-broad 'git add -A' at commit time could pull non-pack files into the CP00-576 closeout commit.
findings.recommendation: Scope the closeout commit to CP00-576 pack files (the active_pack_implementation_refs plus docs/closeout-packs/cp00-576/ artifacts); do not stage the unrelated dirty/untracked files listed under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved.
findings.post_review_disposition: Out-of-scope by design per changed-file-scope.json; committer must avoid staging unrelated files. Non-blocking.
